reasoning_messages.reasoning:

Avoid cp -p but could use cp without -p? The rule says avoid previously tried commands: cp -p /etc/issue /etc/issue.bak and chmod 0644 /etc/issue. We can maybe use install -b to backup? Or use "mv /etc/issue /etc/issue.bak && cp new". But need to edit file directly with echo.

We need to propose plan: read /etc/issue, backup using cp (non -p) maybe allowed? The avoid list only says avoid those exact commands. cp without -p may be okay.

We need full DoD banner text. Provide a reasonable full text. Use write_file to overwrite /etc/issue with the new text after backup. Ensure permissions set to 0644 but not using chmod command directly? Could use write_file with mode parameter to set perms.

Thus plan: read /etc/issue, backup to /etc/issue.bak using run_cmd cp, then write_file new content with mode 0644. That avoids prior commands.

We must submit review_plan.
